summary:
Childhood leukemia survivors who receive cranial radiotherapy face an elevated risk of developing meningiomas due to the known carcinogenic effects of ionizing radiation on brain tissue. This association is well-documented in pediatric oncology studies, with meningioma being one of the most common benign tumors observed post-irradiation. 

Note: The summary focuses only on clinically significant findings related to meningioma incidence among this population based directly from the provided information.
